raw_consumer_statement:
I am enrolled in Wayfair a tried and true program which is 2 free items a month from a specific list of products. I receive the item free as long as I provide a review. The items on this list show free with review so there is not a dollar amount. 
I got a small corduroy couch as normal, wrote the review, like normal, and then received a bill for {$4000.00}. I called and told them this was a mistake and I would never pay that much for that couch. They said yes it look very wrong because that couch was previously listed for {$900.00} and it looks like they charged me 4 times. They sent my call to manager and they said it would be resolved. I received a letter stated it was resolved. I received a new statement showing it was resolved. The following month I received another bill with the XXXX. Ive called multiple times and the same thing happens. They tell me its resolved and its not. This is reflecting negative on my credit report. The last call the associate told me it would be handled and to follow up with tried and true program department which she could not provide me a phone number too. She gave me an email and it was wrong email address. In the beginning, with the first phone call, I told them Id be happy to return the item if they wanted to schedule a pick up too. If for some reason there system was hacked. But I would not pay XXXX for a small love seat that was suppose to be free with review.
